title: anil ovina
parts: Anil ovina has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of Anil ovina are known to contain saponins and glycosides, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves, on the other hand, contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The flowers of Anil ovina contain essential oils and terpenes, which have been used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
side_effects: Anil ovina can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing, as well as gastrointestinal issues like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can also lead to more severe side effects, including liver and kidney damage. In rare cases, anil ovina may interact with other medications, causing adverse reactions like increased heart rate, high blood pressure, and seizures. Improper use of anil ovina can also cause skin irritation, such as redness, rashes, and burns.
